lesson.menu_title: In the Meeting Room -- Starting and Sharing Opinions
lesson.menu_desc: Let's start & What do you think? -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 15 -- In the Meeting Room -- Starting and Sharing Opinions
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Karina Macedo | Aula 15 -- In the Meeting Room: Starting and Sharing Opinions

listenings.text: Good morning, everyone. Thank you for coming to the meeting today. Let us start. The agenda has two points. The first point is the new aircraft, and the second point is the visit next month. I want to hear your opinion on each point, so please share your idea when you are ready. We will make a decision together at the end. Robert, what do you think about the first point?

Hello, this is Robert. I read the agenda for the meeting, and I have a clear opinion. In my opinion, the new aircraft is a very good idea. I agree with the plan, and I think it is the right point to start with. I have one small idea for the second point too. I am ready to share it in the meeting, and I am happy to make a decision together. See you in the meeting room.

extra_audio.text: First, Karina opened the meeting and said let us start. Then she read the agenda with two points. Next, she asked Robert what he thinks about the first point. After that, Robert shared his opinion and said he agrees. Finally, they made a decision together.
